title: Strategic pivot: Hunch repositioned as 'Architectural Conformance for AI code' — the semantic-invariant wedge pattern-SAST structurally can't reach (v0.38.0)
context: 8-stream competitive research concluded the one structurally-defensible, incumbent-proof gap is enforcing SEMANTIC/architectural invariants (layering, must-reach, dependency direction) — which need a graph of intent, not pattern-matching. Semgrep/SonarQube can't express them; Qodo's CEO named the gap ('quality depends on past decisions and tribal knowledge an LLM can't understand'). Hunch already had the engine (src/core/conformance.ts: deterministic graph-reachability over decisions' conformance predicates).
decision: Adopted Architectural Conformance as the headline positioning and built the proof. (1) `hunch conform --add` records an architectural invariant in one line (a decision + a conformance predicate: --assert calls|not-calls|imports|not-imports|exists --subject --object [--transitive] [--why] [--bug]); reuses the existing reachability engine; propagates to all-assistant grounding. (2) `hunch conform --strict` is the CI gate; violations now print a RECEIPT (the decision's why + the bug it prevents reopening) that no pattern-matcher can produce. (3) demo/architectural-conformance.sh — the head-to-head: an AI 'optimizes' a controller to query the DB directly; linter/SAST CLEAN (legit internal import, no bad pattern); Hunch BLOCKED with the receipt. (4) Repositioned package.json, README headline, site hero+title to lead with 'Architectural Conformance for AI code' — memory/recall/capture become supporting machinery.
consequences: This is the GOAT wedge from the research: a moat IN KIND (incumbents can't YAML their way to layering enforcement), built on an engine Hunch already had (fast to defensible, not an R&D moonshot), escaping both unwinnable fights (commoditizing 'memory' and out-Semgrep-ing Semgrep).

The single sellable proof now exists and is verified: 'passes the linter, blocked by Hunch, with the why'. test/conformance.test.ts +1 (controller-bypasses-service-layer flips satisfied→violated); full suite 356 pass; shipped to npm 0.38.0.

Honest open edges to harden next (the research + prior red-teams flag these): conformance currently checks the whole graph state, not the diff — wiring it into the commit/CI buildCheckReport path (block the PR that introduces the violation) is the next step; reachability is over the unified dep graph (edge-type precision: calls vs imports is a later refinement); and the behavioral on/off proof should be re-run on a conformance (architectural) violation, not just a dep import, to get the public number.

Still gated on distribution: the wedge is product-defensible but 'GM' remains a speed/distribution race vs Qodo ($120M) / Semgrep ($204M) converging on semantic enforcement from the pattern+AI-reasoning side. Win condition = own this as the open, git-native, vendor-neutral standard + publish the behavioral number.
